channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Artemis II Astronauts Witnessed 6 Meteorites Colliding With the Moon - WIRED
item.description:
1. Artemis II Astronauts Witnessed 6 Meteorites Colliding With the Moon  WIRED

item.description:

item.description:

item.title: The ‘oldest octopus’ in the world isn’t an octopus after all, scientists find - CNN
item.description:
1. The ‘oldest octopus’ in the world isn’t an octopus after all, scientists find  CNN
  2. "Weird blob" creature thought to be the world's oldest octopus isn't an octopus after all. Here's what scientists found.  CBS News
  3. This Fossil Once Rewrote Everything We Knew About Octopuses. Turns Out, It's Not An Octopus At All  IFLScience
  4. Science to ancient octopus fossil: you’re not an octopus after all  Yahoo
  5. 'Oldest octopus' fossil is no octopus at all, scans reveal  Phys.org

item.title: Artemis II astronauts follow Apollo tradition of naming lunar features after loved ones - AP News

item.title: Is the next 'great comet' coming? See Comet PanSTARRS brighten in the night sky this week. - Live Science
item.description:

item.title: How Artemis II’s Earthset photo compares with the iconic Earthrise image from 1968 - The Conversation
item.description:

item.title: One DNA letter can trigger complete sex reversal - Phys.org
item.description:
1. One DNA letter can trigger complete sex reversal  Phys.org
  2. A single-nucleotide enhancer mutation overrides chromosomal sex to drive XX male development  Nature

  4. Bar-Ilan University Study Reveals Single DNA Letter Can Cause Complete Sex Reversal  Bioengineer.org
  5. One DNA letter can trigger complete sex reversal, Bar-Ilan University study finds (IMAGE)  EurekAlert!

item.title: The lengths male octopuses go to protect the arm they need to mate - Phys.org
item.description:
1. The lengths male octopuses go to protect the arm they need to mate  Phys.org
  2. Male octopus has ‘sex arm’ that can mate in the dark  National Geographic
  3. How octopuses have sex without even seeing each other  Euronews.com
  4. The One Arm an Octopus Will Never Risk in a Fight—Its Sex Arm  ZME Science
  5. A Harvard researcher started studying how octopuses use receptors in their arms to “taste by touch” while hunting prey and exploring. In doing so, his team stumbled on answers to the birds and the bees of cephalopods getting busy. https://wapo.st/4dNeHTR  facebook.com

item.title: This Company Is Launching 4,000 Orbiting ‘Sky Mirrors’ to Beam Sunlight Down to Earth After Dark. Scientists Want It Stopped Now - Indian Defence Review
item.description:

item.title: Mystery giant 'forbidden planet' has been found - and scientists are baffled - Metro.co.uk

item.title: What Is Going On With the Artemis 2 Toilet? - Gizmodo
item.description:

  4. Artemis II’s toilet is a moon mission milestone  Scientific American
